parsed_review.summary:
CP00-742 is a descriptor-only Korean Legal Depth P06 workflow/permission/audit closeout pack (RP24.P06.M03.S11-RP24.P06.M05.S06, risk B, 40 units) that correctly claims production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false. The production_ready=false / descriptor-only claim is fully supported by implementation, tests, local validation, inspection, adjudication, and no-write/no-real-data boundaries.

Coverage: deliverable distribution (ui:8, claude_review:2, security_audit:9, test:8, implementation:13) and micro-title distribution (Primary Implementation Slice:12, Secondary Workflow Slice:22, Permission And Audit Binding:6) each sum to 40 and exactly match KOREAN_LEGAL_CP742_REQUIREMENTS and the prompt baseline. Implementation (registry.js binding/requirements, service.js descriptor/case-set/Hermes/Claude/handoff builders, validators.js) follows the established CP722-CP741 pattern; the CP742 descriptor chains from CP741 and hands off to CP00-743.

Boundaries: KOREAN_LEGAL_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION plus per-row guards keep real client/matter data, raw payloads, credentials, HWPX/court-filing/litigation/corporate/clause runtime, permission-decision detail, audit bodies, and product-state writes closed. validateDescriptor substantively enforces the authority boundary (claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false) and runtime-closed flags; model.test.js independently asserts these for CP00-742 (binding/coverage, runtime-closed descriptor, contract projection latest=CP00-742 with validation.valid=true).

Validation/closeout evidence: command-evidence records all ten required commands (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) at exit_code 0; the regenerated contract shows validation.valid=true with cp742 coverage+descriptor errors=[]. Plan live_state, manifest plan_binding_snapshot, and next-pack-queue all agree on CP00-742.

Review hardening (CP178+): readiness auth preflight passed with non-zero (1432-byte) valid-JSON stdout and exit 0; no forbidden review evidence (no auth failure, 0-byte stdout, malformed or fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, or missing fields). Claude is confined to Read/Grep/Glob, read-only and non-final; Hermes patterns are used only for the deterministic descriptor_only_korean_legal_no_write review packet/receipt (emits_runtime_receipt=false, hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false), not product runtime. Scope hygiene is correct: unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, workbook docs, LDIP review artifact) are excluded and no active untracked implementation files are omitted.

The PENDING values in claude-review-result.json and construction-inspection.json are the expected pre-review placeholder state that this review run populates, not defects. No P0-P3 findings; the pack closeout goal is not blocked.
